title: Point2D Object
description: Transient 2D point. A transient point is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient 2D points are used as a wrapper to work with raw 2D point information. They are created statically using the create methods of the Point2D class.

methods.name: asArray
methods.description: Get coordinate data of the point

methods.name: asVector
methods.description: Defines a vector using the coordinates of the point.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns a copy of this point object.

methods.name: distanceTo
methods.description: Returns the distance from this point to another point.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets the data defining the point.

methods.name: isEqualTo
methods.description: Checks to see if this point and another point are equal (have identical coordinates). The comparison is done within the modeling tolerance which can be found with the Application.pointTolerance property. If you want to compare two points with any other tolerance you can use the isEqualToByTolerance method.

methods.name: isEqualToByTolerance
methods.description: Checks to see if this point and another point are equal within the specified tolerance.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets the coordinates of the point by specifying the x, y coordinates.

methods.name: setWithArray
methods.description: Sets the coordinates of the point using an array as input.

methods.name: transformBy
methods.description: Transforms the point using the provided matrix.

methods.name: translateBy
methods.description: Translates the point using the provided vector.

methods.name: vectorTo
methods.description: Returns a vector from this point to another point.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: x
properties.description: Gets and sets the X coordinate of the point.

properties.name: y
properties.description: Gets and sets the Y coordinate of the point.

asArray.title: Point2D.asArray Method
asArray.description: Get coordinate data of the point
asArray.return_description: Returns the coordinate data of the point as an array

asVector.title: Point2D.asVector Method
asVector.description: Defines a vector using the coordinates of the point.

classType.title: Point2D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Point2D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns a copy of this point object.
copy.return_description: Returns an independent copy of this point object.

create.title: Point2D.create Method

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.core.Point2D.create()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.core.Point2D.create(x, y)

create.return_description: Returns the new Point2D object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The x coordinate of the point   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.0.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The y coordinate of the point   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.0.

distanceTo.title: Point2D.distanceTo Method
distanceTo.description: Returns the distance from this point to another point.
distanceTo.return_description: Returns the distance to the point.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to measure the distance to

getData.title: Point2D.getData Method
getData.description: Gets the data defining the point.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The output x coordinate of the point.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The output y coordinate of the point.

isEqualTo.title: Point2D.isEqualTo Method
isEqualTo.description: Checks to see if this point and another point are equal (have identical coordinates). The comparison is done within the modeling tolerance which can be found with the Application.pointTolerance property. If you want to compare two points with any other tolerance you can use the isEqualToByTolerance method.
isEqualTo.return_description: Returns true if the points are equal. (have identical coordinates)

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to compare for equality

isEqualToByTolerance.title: Point2D.isEqualToByTolerance Method
isEqualToByTolerance.description: Checks to see if this point and another point are equal within the specified tolerance.
isEqualToByTolerance.return_description: Returns true if the points are equal (have identical coordinates).

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to compare for equality.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: The tolerance, in centimeters, to use when comparing the two points.

set.title: Point2D.set Method
set.description: Sets the coordinates of the point by specifying the x, y coordinates.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The x coordinate of the point.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The y coordinate of the point.

setWithArray.title: Point2D.setWithArray Method
setWithArray.description: Sets the coordinates of the point using an array as input.

parameters.name: coordinates
parameters.description: An array that defines the coordinates of the point

transformBy.title: Point2D.transformBy Method
transformBy.description: Transforms the point using the provided matrix.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: The Matrix2D object that defines the transformation

translateBy.title: Point2D.translateBy Method
translateBy.description: Translates the point using the provided vector.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to use to translate the point

vectorTo.title: Point2D.vectorTo Method
vectorTo.description: Returns a vector from this point to another point.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The other point to use to create the vector

isValid.title: Point2D.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = point2D_var.isValid

objectType.title: Point2D.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = point2D_var.objectType

x.title: Point2D.x Property
x.description: Gets and sets the X coordinate of the point.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = point2D_var.x

# Set the value of the property.
point2D_var.x = propertyValue

y.title: Point2D.y Property
y.description: Gets and sets the Y coordinate of the point.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = point2D_var.y

# Set the value of the property.
point2D_var.y = propertyValue
